description: Calibration assumptions for daily invalidation scan. VLA assumptions are research-grade (具身智能研究员视角). AI Agent assumptions track developer/enterprise AI. System assumptions monitor pipeline health.

assumptions.hypothesis: Daily reports should remain freshness-first: prioritize last 72h, allow up to 7d with explicit labeling.
invalidation_conditions: The daily output includes items older than 30 days without explicit '旧闻补充' labeling and a justification.
Multiple consecutive days show repeated 'news' items with no new primary source update.

assumptions.hypothesis: Key claims in daily outputs should be grounded in primary sources (official blog / GitHub release / docs).
invalidation_conditions: A daily output contains a high-value claim with only a secondary source and no primary URL.
A daily output includes a non-existent product/model announcement (hallucinated) as a headline.

assumptions.hypothesis: Diffusion-class action generation (Diffusion Policy, flow matching / pi0, ACT-style chunking) remains the dominant VLA action head paradigm for top-venue papers through 2026. Autoregressive tokenized-action approaches (like ARP, UniPi) will not surpass it on SIMPLER/LIBERO benchmarks without architectural novelty.
invalidation_conditions: Two or more papers from different labs demonstrate that a non-diffusion action head (autoregressive tokens, energy-based, etc.) beats diffusion-class by >10% success rate on SIMPLER or LIBERO-Spatial at the same parameter count.
Physical Intelligence (pi.ai), Google DeepMind, or CMU Robotics explicitly announces deprecation of diffusion/flow-matching action head in their main production model.
CoRL or RSS 2026 best paper award goes to a non-diffusion VLA architecture that substantially outperforms current SOTA.

assumptions.hypothesis: Tactile/force sensing will transition from research accessory to necessary modality in high-precision VLA manipulation pipelines by end of 2026. Tasks like peg-in-hole, cable routing, deformable object assembly, and grasp force control will require tactile to surpass 80% success rate.
invalidation_conditions: 6 consecutive months (by RSS + SOTA tracker) with no top-venue VLA paper featuring tactile/force sensing as a core contribution, across CoRL/ICRA/RSS/NeurIPS.
A paper demonstrates vision-only VLA (no tactile/force input) achieves >85% success on AnyManip or INSERT benchmark under industrial tolerance (<1mm), without any sensing modality beyond RGB.
Leading robot hardware companies (Unitree, Figure, 1X) explicitly remove haptic/tactile sensing from their next-gen hardware roadmap.

assumptions.hypothesis: GPU-parallel simulation (Isaac Lab / Genesis) + domain randomization remains the primary scalable data source for VLA contact-rich manipulation training. Real-robot-only labs will face a data bottleneck that sim-trained methods will outpace within 12 months.
invalidation_conditions: A top-venue paper from a real-data-only lab (no sim) outperforms the best sim-trained VLA by >20% on a non-cherry-picked manipulation benchmark with >50 diverse tasks.
Isaac Lab or Genesis releases a critical bug report showing contact simulation is systematically off for rigid-body manipulation, leading to major labs abandoning it.
NVIDIA or MIT publicly abandons GPU-parallel sim in favor of internet-video pretraining as the primary data strategy for contact-rich manipulation.

assumptions.hypothesis: Humanoid robots (Unitree G1/H1, Figure 02, 1X NEO, Agility Digit, Fourier GR-1) will achieve production-quality VLA control in structured factory/warehouse environments by end of 2026: >4h continuous autonomous operation on a defined task set without human intervention.
invalidation_conditions: No humanoid company publishes a verifiable case study of VLA-controlled humanoid running autonomously for >4h in a real production environment (warehouse, auto factory, fulfillment center) by Dec 2026.
Three or more humanoid companies (Figure, 1X, Unitree, Fourier) revert from VLA-based control to scripted FSM/motion planning as their primary production control method.
A major humanoid hardware company shuts down or pivots away from general-purpose manipulation entirely.

assumptions.hypothesis: Online RL fine-tuning of VLA policies (DPPO, GRPO-style, or equivalent) will become the dominant post-training paradigm by mid-2026, showing >15% improvement in out-of-distribution success rate over behavior-cloning-only baselines on at least 2 major benchmarks.
invalidation_conditions: By CoRL 2026 deadline, fewer than 15% of accepted manipulation papers include RL/RLHF post-training experiments as a core contribution.
A meta-analysis paper (>3 labs, >10 tasks) shows RL fine-tuning of VLAs systematically degrades OOD generalization with success rate drop >10% on held-out objects, with no effective mitigation.
OpenAI, Anthropic, or Google publicly releases evidence that RLHF/RL degrades physical manipulation grounding in their VLA research.

assumptions.hypothesis: World models (UniSim, GR-1, SuSIE, DreamerV3-based) will graduate from auxiliary imagination tools to first-class planning components in VLA by end of 2026, placing world-model-first architectures in the top-5 of SIMPLER or equivalent long-horizon benchmark.
invalidation_conditions: No world-model-integrated VLA appears in top-5 of SIMPLER Bench, OXE Eval, or RoboVerse leaderboard by Dec 2026.
An influential ICLR/NeurIPS 2026 paper demonstrates that world model complexity in VLA adds <3% success rate improvement on real robot tasks at matched parameter budget.
Google DeepMind or MIT CSAIL publicly abandons latent world model components in their VLA research line.

assumptions.hypothesis: Human teleoperation (ALOHA 2, UMI, Mobile ALOHA, AgiBot) will remain the irreplaceable primary data collection method for contact-rich dexterous manipulation through 2026. Internet-video pretraining will supplement but not replace it.
invalidation_conditions: A top-venue paper demonstrates a VLA trained with zero human teleoperation (only internet video + sim data) achieves >60% success on a diverse 50-task real-robot benchmark comparable to ALOHA-trained policies.
Three or more major labs publicly announce they have completely replaced teleoperation with autonomous self-supervised collection or video pretraining as their primary training data strategy.

assumptions.hypothesis: Open-source VLA weights (OpenVLA, Octo 2, lerobot-based, pi0 variants) will maintain competitive capability within 6-month lag of closed-source frontier (pi.ai, Google RT, NVIDIA), keeping research community accessible to published techniques.
invalidation_conditions: For 6+ consecutive months, best open-source VLA lags best closed-source VLA by >30 percentage points on SIMPLER or LIBERO-Spatial benchmark with no new open-source release in sight.

Physical Intelligence or Google DeepMind announce permanent restrictions preventing replication of their VLA architecture in open-source settings (e.g., proprietary sensor required, hardware lock-in).

assumptions.hypothesis: Cross-embodiment zero-shot transfer of VLA policies remains hard: a model trained on one morphology (single-arm 6-DOF) will not generalize beyond 50% to a significantly different morphology (bimanual, mobile, dexterous hand) without task-specific fine-tuning.
invalidation_conditions: A peer-reviewed paper demonstrates >70% zero-shot cross-embodiment transfer from a single-arm 6-DOF robot to a bimanual or mobile manipulation robot on a standardized 10-task eval suite without any fine-tuning.
OXE (Open X-Embodiment) or equivalent joint-training study shows cross-embodiment generalization above 60% success rate on held-out robot morphologies that were not in training data.

assumptions.hypothesis: 3D spatial understanding (depth, point clouds, NeRF/Gaussian Splat representations) will become necessary components for fine manipulation VLA by mid-2026. Pure 2D RGB-only representations will plateau below 70% on standardized precision tasks (sub-1cm accuracy).
invalidation_conditions: SOTA VLA using only 2D RGB input (no depth, no 3D representation) achieves top-3 on SIMPLER Bench or LIBERO-Spatial after June 2026, without using any implicit 3D-aware backbone.
Google Robotics or MIT publishes a position paper arguing 3D representations are unnecessary and 2D suffices for all manipulation tasks of interest, backed by large-scale empirical evidence.

assumptions.hypothesis: MCP and interoperable tool protocols become de facto standard for AI agent tool integration, with major IDEs/platforms providing native support by end of 2026.
invalidation_conditions: Two or more of OpenAI/Anthropic/Google announce incompatible private tool protocols as their primary solution.
Critical security vulnerability found in MCP causing enterprise platforms to withdraw support at scale.

assumptions.hypothesis: Agentic coding (AI autonomously writes, tests, and ships code) will reach 80% success rate on junior-level programming tasks with no human intervention by end of 2026.
invalidation_conditions: SWE-bench Verified top score remains below 60% by year-end with no clear upward trend.
Multiple large tech companies publicly report AI coding agents have significantly higher production bug rates than humans and reduce usage.

assumptions.hypothesis: Multi-agent collaboration frameworks will mature from lab tools to engineering practice, with production-grade frameworks (>1k GitHub stars) showing real performance gains over single agents.
invalidation_conditions: 6 consecutive months with no breakthrough release from any multi-agent framework.
Influential engineering report shows multi-agent architectures cost/complexity significantly exceeds benefits in real production systems.

assumptions.hypothesis: Reasoning models (extended thinking, chain-of-thought) will show persistent advantages in AI agent tasks, becoming standard for agent planning and decision-making.
invalidation_conditions: Benchmark study proves that for 90% of AI agent tasks, reasoning models show no significant advantage over base chat models when latency is normalized.
Leading reasoning models (o3, R1, DeepSeek) stagnate on agent benchmarks for 6+ months.

assumptions.hypothesis: AI workflow automation becomes the fastest-growing enterprise AI deployment scenario in 2026, surpassing pure chat/QA applications.
invalidation_conditions: Year-end Gartner/Forrester enterprise AI report shows workflow automation is not in top-3 high-growth scenarios.
Core commercial use cases for OpenAI/Anthropic/Google show workflow automation falling below 20% of revenue mix.

assumptions.hypothesis: Open-weight models (Qwen, LLaMA, Gemma) will match 80% of closed-source model capabilities on AI agent tasks by end of 2026, making them the engineering default due to cost advantages.
invalidation_conditions: LMSYS Arena or equivalent year-end data shows open-weight models still >30% behind closed-source leaders on agent task success rate.
Qwen/LLaMA series stagnate on agent benchmarks (AgentBench/WebArena) for 6+ months with no new version breakthrough.

assumptions.hypothesis: This automation pipeline maintains stable daily output; Watchdog re-run mechanism covers >90% of single-point failures without human intervention.
invalidation_conditions: 3 consecutive days of unrecoverable report gaps that watchdog cannot auto-repair.
After 30 days of operation, daily average output drops below 50% of first-week baseline (excluding API outages/holidays).
